target.title: AI-assisted: fix(chat): bound chat history display payloads
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes `chat.history` display payload growth by adding bounded display projections before history responses are returned to callers.

It covers text, tool payloads, image/media blocks, canvas media references, Swift protocol surfaces, UI/controller handling, gateway tests, and public protocol docs. The branch has been rebased and retargeted onto the current `release/2026.5.26` line and includes the later P2 raw-mode alignment fix.

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex produced the implementation and PR publication using OpenAI Codex `gpt-5.5` in the OpenClaw workspace. Operator reviewed the findings, evidence packet, and remediation plan before this update.

## Problem

`chat.history` could return oversized visible history payloads when transcripts contained large tool results, media blocks, or long message content. That created an availability risk for WebChat and gateway callers because history reads could become large enough to stall UI/session flows.

A late review pass also found that normal `messages` mode was capped to 64 KiB after safe tool redaction, which could unnecessarily drop safe text even when the caller configured a larger history budget.

## Root Cause

History projection and redaction were not consistently separated from raw transcript preservation. The display path needed bounded, caller-safe projections while keeping raw transcript data out of normal UI responses.

The final `messages` mode budget also applied a hard 64 KiB cap after redaction, rather than honoring the configured history budget for safe text.

## Solution

- Adds bounded chat display projection helpers for text, tool payloads, media, and canvas blocks.
- Routes `chat.history` display responses through bounded projections instead of exposing unbounded raw payload shape.
- Adds `mode: "messages" | "turns" | "raw-messages"` and gates raw payload inspection behind `operator.admin`.
- Keeps `unsafeRawToolPayloads` schema-compatible, but it does not unlock raw payloads without explicit `mode: "raw-messages"`.
- Preserves safe `messages` mode text up to the configured `maxHistoryBytes` budget after tool payload omission.
- Regenerates Swift protocol models from the canonical schema output.
- Documents the new history modes and raw-history admin guard in public WebChat/Gateway protocol docs.
- Adds gateway regression tests for bounded tool payloads, visible media shaping, recent visible turn preservation, placeholder semantics, large safe text preservation, saturated scan-window metadata, and raw-mode authorization.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed:** `chat.history` was returning oversized display payloads. Production logs showed >=88 KB responses and `readMs=1715-1893` even for tiny sessions, plus an 11050 ms WebSocket response. Normal display history could also expose raw tool-call arguments or tool-result payloads. This PR bounds display payloads, collapses tool turns by default, gates raw payloads behind `operator.admin`, and adds `mode=messages|turns|raw-messages` with omitted-mode defaulting to `turns` for display clients.

**Current patch tested:** `b169090c0924c2be716b8eb6872ca79fe6964c47`, based on `e0bc8efde3ef7348b9ff5b8f48e9515e670fcb65` from `release/2026.5.26`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
corepack pnpm tsgo:core
corepack pnpm exec vitest run \
  src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts \
  src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts \
  ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts \
  ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts \
  test/scripts/list-prod-store-packages.test.ts
git diff --check origin/release/2026.5.26...HEAD
```

**Observed result after this patch:**

```text
corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile: PASS
corepack pnpm tsgo:core: PASS
Focused chat-history suite: PASS, 8 files, 395 tests
git diff --check origin/release/2026.5.26...HEAD: PASS
Current scoped diff: 17 files, 2083 insertions, 68 deletions
Dependency-related files in current scoped diff: 0
GitHub PR base: release/2026.5.26
```

**Raw-mode authorization behavior verified on current patch stack:**

The focused suite includes the raw-mode and compatibility regressions for:

```text
chat.history messages mode omits raw tool payloads
chat.history messages mode ignores unsafeRawToolPayloads for admin callers
chat.history raw-messages requires an admin or debug caller
chat.history raw-messages preserves raw tool payloads
```

Expected behavior remains:

```text
mode=messages + unsafeRawToolPayloads=true -> safe display projection, raw tool payloads omitted
mode=raw-messages without operator.admin -> INVALID_REQUEST
mode=raw-messages with operator.admin -> raw diagnostic transcript path
```

**Live/dogfood telemetry from the affected surface:**

Post-restart `chat.history` telemetry showed display clients using the bounded turns path:

```text
mode=turns responseMode=turns rawToolPayloads=false
```

Representative large-history sample after the beta1 rebase:

```text
sessionHash=52d4d0e214e0
mode=turns responseMode=turns rawToolPayloads=false
requestedLimit=20
localMessages=412
returnedItems=10
toolRecordsCollapsed=395
readFileBytes=1130353
responseBytesReturned=29150
readMs=13
totalMs=21
hasMoreBefore=false
```

Post-hotfix sampled window:

```text
samples: 50
totalMs: min 0, p50 3, p90 24, max 605
readMs:  min 0, p50 1, p90 14, max 593
responseBytesReturned: min 212, p50 2690, p90 29150, max 29150
readFileBytes: min 1400, p50 5076, p90 1130353, max 1130353
toolRecordsCollapsed: min 0, p50 2, p90 395, max 395
```

Two roughly 600 ms outliers remained in the checked window. The new telemetry attributes those to read-path components while response size stays bounded. This is evidence that the display payload/serialization outage path is fixed, not a claim that all filesystem/read jitter is solved by this PR.

Production before-evidence from the affected surface, redacted to session hashes, from the May 24 OpenClaw logs:

```text
2026-05-24T23:13:35.952Z chat.history metrics mode=messages sessionHash=ac38af406fed ... messagesBytesReturned=87915 responseBytesReturned=88104 readMs=1838 totalMs=1840
2026-05-24T23:13:35.947Z chat.history metrics mode=messages sessionHash=75794a8e1d45 ... messagesBytesReturned=57617 responseBytesReturned=57810 readMs=1890 totalMs=1893
2026-05-24T23:01:46.498Z chat.history metrics mode=messages sessionHash=a6923f4da653 ... localMessages=0 messagesBytesReturned=2 responseBytesReturned=128 readMs=1715 totalMs=1716
2026-05-24T06:19:28.690Z WebSocket chat.history response took 11050ms
```

**Observed result after fix:** Display clients return `mode=turns` by default, collapse tool activity, bound response bytes, and do not expose raw tool payloads. Explicit `mode=messages` remains message-shaped and display-safe. Non-admin `mode=raw-messages` rejects with `INVALID_REQUEST`. Admin `mode=raw-messages` is the explicit raw diagnostic path. Admin callers sending `mode: "messages", unsafeRawToolPayloads: true` still get the safe display projection with tool payloads omitted.

**Not tested:** Control UI / WebChat browser surface was not driven through a browser or screen-recording session in this proof. Compatibility was exercised at the Gateway WebSocket protocol layer and through UI controller/app tests.

## Validation

| Command / Gate | Result | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| 2026.5.26 current-tip rebase | PASS | Current head `b169090c09` is based on `e0bc8efde3` |
| `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` | PASS | Current rebased patch stack |
| Focused chat-history suite | PASS | 8 files, 395 tests on current rebased patch stack |
| 2026.5.26 diff whitespace check | PASS | `git diff --check origin/release/2026.5.26...HEAD` |
| Dependency scope check | PASS | No package, lockfile, or shrinkwrap files in current scoped diff |
| Swift protocol generation | PASS | `GatewayModels.swift` regenerated to canonical output |

## Review Findings Addressed

- ClawSweeper real-behavior-proof block: PR body now includes the exact current-head command field, current-head test result, and raw-mode authorization proof.
- ClawSweeper P2 docs finding: documented `chat.history` modes and raw-history admin guard in `docs/web/webchat.md` and `docs/gateway/protocol.md`.
- ClawSweeper P3 stale beta report finding: removed `docs/plan/chat-history-beta2-fix-report-2026-05-25.md` from the PR.
- Bundled Swift protocol check: regenerated `GatewayModels.swift` instead of hand-editing optional defaults.
- Agent-chat tests: updated the gateway-callable helper expectations so placeholder redaction does not break semantic assertions.
- Dependency/deadcode check: current scoped diff has no dependency-related files and current-head diff hygiene was rerun.
- Copilot hot-path nits: avoided a second `augmentChatHistoryWithCanvasBlocks()` pass for non-raw history responses and cached `extractToolCallIds()` in turn aggregation.
- Saturated scan-window `meta.hasMoreBefore` handling: fixed with a 1001-turn regression test verifying earlier history is reported when the scan window saturates.
- ClawSweeper P2 `unsafeRawToolPayloads` contract: raw-payload branch now requires `mode === "raw-messages"`. Admin callers using `mode: "messages"` with the flag get the safe display projection.

## Non-goals and Follow-Up

This PR intentionally does not address Codex/WebChat message-tool delivery hints, Codex pretool/tool-loop latency, provider failover, gateway startup auth prewarm, or reconnect/warmup read coalescing. Those have separate symptoms and should remain separate PRs.

Browser visual proof can be added if maintainers want it, but the current proof covers Gateway protocol behavior, UI controller/app behavior, current-head regression tests, and live dogfood telemetry for the bounded display path.

## Risk Before Merge

- Compatibility: `chat.history` response shape is intentionally bounded for display modes. Raw/debug access remains behind the explicit admin raw path.
- Availability: this reduces large-history response pressure, but maintainers should still watch WebChat history latency on beta/release builds.
- Session-state: history display output changes should be checked against existing UI expectations and Swift clients.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` entry was added. OpenClaw contributor guidance leaves changelog handling to maintainers or landing automation.

target.title: fix(diagnostics): populate inputMessages/outputMessages on model.call events
target.body:
## Summary

Populates `inputMessages`/`outputMessages` on model-call diagnostic events so that the `diagnostics-otel` plugin can export raw model content to OTEL backends when `captureContent` is enabled.

Related to #77391 (model-call diagnostic content capture only; toolInputs/toolOutputs remain out of scope for this PR and should stay tracked separately).

## Problem

The `DiagnosticModelCallBaseEvent` type documents `inputMessages` and `outputMessages` fields, and the OTEL plugin reads them via `assignOtelModelContentAttributes()`, but the core model-call wrapper never populated them. This meant `openclaw.content.input_messages` and `openclaw.content.output_messages` were always absent from exported OTEL spans regardless of `captureContent` configuration.

## Changes

### 1. Extract inputMessages from onPayload (not model argument)
The stream function's first argument is a provider descriptor, not the request payload. The actual messages array is only available in the `onPayload(payload, model)` callback after provider wrappers have mutated it. Input messages are now captured at this seam and stored in `ModelCallObservationState.inputMessages`.

### 2. Handle normalized stream chunks in output extraction
The runtime normalizes all provider streams to `{ type: "text_delta", delta }` before they reach the diagnostic observer. `extractTextFromChunk()` now checks normalized formats (text_delta, text) **before** raw OpenAI wire formats, so most real model streams will capture output content.

### 3. Gate content by captureContent policy before emission
Added `ContentCapturePolicy` to `ModelCallDiagnosticContext` with a `resolveContentCapturePolicy()` helper (mirrors the OTEL service's logic). `inputMessages`/`outputMessages` are only populated on diagnostic events when the corresponding policy flag is enabled. This prevents raw prompts from traveling the internal diagnostic bus when the operator has not opted in.

### 4. Tests
- All existing test fixtures updated with required `contentCapture` field.
- New test cases:
  - "respects contentCapture policy - omits inputMessages when disabled"
  - "respects contentCapture policy - omits outputMessages when disabled"
  - "captures output from normalized text_delta chunks"
- 11/11 tests passing.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Diagnostic model-call events did not carry `inputMessages`/`outputMessages`, so the OTEL plugin could never export raw model content to backends like Langfuse even when `captureContent` was enabled.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw gateway on macOS (Node 22), built from PR branch. OTEL exporter configured to send to Langfuse pre-production collector at `trace-pre.agent.kspmas.ksyun.com`.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
1. Build PR branch: `pnpm build`
2. Replace installed OpenClaw dist with PR build
3. Restart gateway with OTEL exporter + `captureContent.enabled=true`
4. Send a test message: `openclaw agent -m "Say hello in one word" --agent main`
5. Query Langfuse API for the latest `openclaw.model.call` observation
6. Set `captureContent.enabled=false`, restart, send another message
7. Query Langfuse API again to verify content attributes are absent

**Evidence after fix**:

With `captureContent` enabled, querying Langfuse API shows content present:
```
$ python3 -c "...query Langfuse API..."
=== openclaw.model.call | trace:e5991835cfb229485ec26ce95816b4d5 | ts:2026-05-09T16:17:53.96 ===
  input_messages present: True
  input_messages (first 100): You are a personal assistant running inside OpenClaw.
  output_messages present: True
  output_messages: 哈喽！
```

With `captureContent` disabled, content attributes are absent:
```
=== openclaw.model.call | trace:b6d8900f776750c4 | ts:2026-05-09T16:10:45.54 ===
  openclaw.api = openai-completions
  openclaw.model = glm-5.1
  openclaw.model_call.request_bytes = 99725
  openclaw.model_call.response_bytes = 1600
  *** HAS CONTENT: False
```

**Observed result after fix**: The `openclaw.model.call` span now carries `openclaw.content.input_messages` and `openclaw.content.output_messages` attributes when `captureContent` is enabled, and omits them when disabled. The policy gate works end-to-end through the core wrapper, diagnostic event bus, and OTEL exporter.

**What was not tested**: Swift/macOS app build (unrelated to this change); OTEL backends other than Langfuse.

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Control UI could restore a persisted historical routed session key, such as direct, group, dm, per-peer, or threaded routed session forms, after a reload.
- Why it matters: reopening Control UI could land the user in a stale routed session instead of the real main session, making the UI appear misrouted while the backend remained pointed at main.
- Intended outcome: settings restore sanitizes historical routed session keys and falls back to main before the restored session state is used.
- Out of scope: this does not change gateway routing, heartbeat targeting, live session switching, or backend session semantics.
- Reviewer focus: the routed-session key predicate in ui/src/ui/storage.ts and the storage restore regression cases in ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts.

## Linked context

Closes #61661

Related PRs or discussions: none beyond the linked issue and this PR's ClawSweeper review history.

Maintainer or owner request: not a direct maintainer request; this is a focused bug fix for the linked routed-session restore issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI persisted settings could restore historical routed direct/group/dm/per-peer/threaded session keys instead of falling back to main.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of openclaw/openclaw on Linux against the PR branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
  - git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
  - git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts covers these after-fix restore outcomes:
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed group session
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-peer routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a threaded per-peer routed session
  - falls back to main when restoring a threaded routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a legacy threaded dm session
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-account-channel-peer routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed group session with thread suffix
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-account routed group session with topic suffix

  Latest current-head validation reported on the PR:
  - storage restore test file passed: 33 tests
  - check:test-types passed
  - targeted agentic files from the failed CI job passed: 212 tests
  - git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD passed
  - git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main passed
- Observed result after fix: persisted historical routed session selections are normalized to main during Control UI settings restore; non-historical agent main session keys remain valid.
- What was not tested: browser screenshot/video proof and live backend routing, because the fix is limited to the local settings restore boundary and does not change runtime routing.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof is from local branch validation and copied terminal output, not a full browser session recording.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-fix storage restore trusted persisted routed session keys and had no restore regression coverage for these historical routed forms.

## Tests and validation

Commands run for this body repair and live-state validation:

- git fetch upstream --prune
- git fetch origin --prune
- git merge-tree --write-tree origin/fix/control-ui-routed-session-restore upstream/main
- authenticated GitHub API read of PR #78741 and current base-branch .github/pull_request_template.md
- authenticated GitHub API update of PR #78741 body
- authenticated GitHub API re-read of PR #78741 body headings

Current-head branch validation already captured for the code change:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
- CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
- git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
- git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main

Regression coverage added or updated: ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts now covers routed direct, routed group, per-peer direct, threaded per-peer, threaded direct, legacy threaded dm, per-account direct, group thread suffix, and per-account group topic suffix restore cases.

What failed before this fix, if known: historical routed session keys could be restored from persisted Control UI settings instead of being discarded back to main.

If no test was added, why not: not applicable; focused storage restore tests were added and expanded.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (Yes/No)

Yes. Reloaded Control UI sessions now fall back to main when the persisted value is a historical routed session key.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (Yes/No)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (Yes/No)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The key-shape classifier could accidentally treat a valid persisted session key as historical.

How is that risk mitigated?

The predicate is scoped to routed direct/group/dm/thread forms, and regression tests cover both sanitized historical shapes and valid non-historical agent main session keys.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper and maintainers can re-check the PR body against the current upstream template and continue review of the existing code change.

Still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof: no known author-side action remains for this template-body repair after this update; maintainer or bot re-evaluation may still be needed.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: this body update addresses the current-template PR body gap by replacing the older expanded template with the current top-level sections: Summary, Linked context, Real behavior proof, Tests and validation, Risk checklist, and Current review state.

target.title: fix(gateway): drop pre-session-start subagent_announce pairs from chat.history hydration
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: After `/new` on a chat (feishu in the reporter's trace) that previously received a `subagent_announce` delivery under the same `sessionKey`, the runtime's auto-call of `sessions_history(sessionKey=…)` — which proxies through `chat.history` — hydrates the new conversation with the trailing **assistant reply** from the long-finished announce turn. The model treats it as "the most recent thing the user just said" and resumes an unrelated topic. (#85648)
- **Root Cause**: `chat-display-projection.ts` already hides the user-role inter-session announce via `isSubagentAnnounceInterSessionUserMessage`, but only the user message — its assistant reply has no announce-specific signal of its own and falls through to the chat-history response. That contract is intentional inside an active session (see `session-history-state.test.ts` "drops subagent announce inter-session user messages from projected history": the assistant reply is the agent's takeaway from the announce and is real mid-session content). After `/new` resets the session, however, the same assistant reply is *stale* context that the model should not see. The existing per-message projection cannot tell the two cases apart — both look identical from a projection-only perspective.
- **Fix**: Use `entry.sessionStartedAt` (already bumped to `now` by `/new` and silent rotations in `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts:633` and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.ts:62`) as a hard recency cutoff inside `chat.history`. Drop a `subagent_announce` inter-session user message **plus its immediately-adjacent assistant reply** when the user message's record timestamp predates the cutoff; leave mid-session announces (timestamp ≥ cutoff) and the projection contract untouched.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts`: in `parsedSessionEntryToMessage`, forward the outer JSONL record's `timestamp` (ISO or numeric) to `__openclaw.recordTimestampMs` so downstream filters can compare per-message recency without re-reading the transcript.
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`: add `dropPreSessionStartAnnouncePairs(messages, sessionStartedAt)` helper (uses `normalizeInputProvenance` already imported into this file and `asOptionalRecord` from `shared/record-coerce.ts`) and call it in the `chat.history` handler between `readRecentSessionMessagesAsync` and `augmentChatHistoryWithCliSessionImports`.
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`: six unit tests for the helper (pre-cutoff pair stripped with surrounding turns intact, mid-session pair preserved, undefined `sessionStartedAt` no-op, trailing pre-cutoff user-only fall-through, normal pre-cutoff user/assistant not stripped, missing record timestamp not stripped).
  - `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts`: one test confirming the reader attaches `recordTimestampMs` to `__openclaw` for outer-timestamped records.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `chat-display-projection.ts` is **untouched**. The existing `session-history-state.test.ts` contract (mid-session announce keeps the assistant reply visible) is preserved.
  - No config surface changed (no new key, no default, no schema, no doctor migration).
  - No plugin surface changed (no plugin SDK / manifest / extensions / `api.ts` / `runtime-api.ts` / registry / public-surface-loader edits).
  - No transcript persistence path, no session-store entry resolution, no `chat.history` sessionKey → sessionId lookup, no announce-delivery runner change. Other readers/consumers of `readRecentSessionMessagesAsync` see one additional optional field on `__openclaw` and nothing else.
  - **No transcript backfill needed**: `__openclaw.recordTimestampMs` is attached at parse time by `parsedSessionEntryToMessage`, not stored in the JSONL. The outer record `timestamp` field it reads has been written by `appendEntry` / `ensureTranscriptHeader` since the format existed, so every existing transcript already exposes the value once this PR ships. If a legacy or malformed record has no outer `timestamp` at all, `readChatHistoryRecordTimestampMs` returns `undefined` and the filter is a no-op for that message (failure-open) — matching the existing chat-display-projection contract.
  - **Idle-reset supplementary sub-case in issue #85648 comment thread is NOT covered**: a Telegram report describes ordinary (non-announce) yesterday-messages bleeding into a fresh post-idle-reset session via "Conversation context (untrusted, chronological, selected for current message)" injection **at prompt-compile time** — a different code path that does not pass through `chat.history`. This PR's filter is gated on `subagent_announce` provenance and only touches the `chat.history` hydration path, so that supplementary sub-case is intentionally out of scope; the linked issue should stay open (hence `Refs`, not `Fixes`).

### Reproduction

1. From a feishu (or any) direct chat, spawn a subagent that emits `subagent_announce`. The announce turn is persisted under the same `sessionKey` as the user's main session.
2. Days later, issue `/new` on that chat (which bumps `entry.sessionStartedAt` to `now` and mints a fresh `sessionId`).
3. The runtime auto-calls `sessions_history(sessionKey=…)` shortly after `session.started`.
- Before: `chat.history` returns the announce assistant reply at `seq=2` (alongside or instead of the user announce at `seq=1`); the model resumes the unrelated subagent topic in the fresh paper-defense conversation.
- After: any `subagent_announce` user message whose record timestamp predates the new `sessionStartedAt` is dropped together with its adjacent assistant reply; the new session is hydrated only with current-session turns (or empty).

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed (#85648)**: `chat.history` (and therefore the `sessions_history` agent tool) must not hydrate a freshly `/new`'d session with the assistant reply from a stale, pre-cutoff `subagent_announce` turn under the same `sessionKey`; mid-session announce assistant replies must still come through; non-announce messages must never be stripped by this path.
- **Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22)**:
  1. Targeted Vitest run against the real `parsedSessionEntryToMessage`/`readRecentSessionMessagesAsync` (real reader, real `__openclaw` attach) and the real `dropPreSessionStartAnnouncePairs` helper (the same code path the `chat.history` handler invokes between message read and `augmentChatHistoryWithCliSessionImports`).
  2. Standalone end-to-end repro: real on-disk JSONL transcript + real `readRecentSessionMessagesAsync` reader (production filesystem path) + real `dropPreSessionStartAnnouncePairs` filter, driven through the same data flow the `chat.history` handler uses.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. `node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts`
  2. `node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/qmd85648/repro.mts` (the harness writes a realistic `0015f22f-….jsonl` containing a `subagent_announce` user message + its assistant reply + a fresh post-/new user turn, then reads it through the real gateway reader and applies the real filter with `entry.sessionStartedAt` set to the post-/new cutoff).
- **Evidence after fix (verbatim Vitest summary + verbatim end-to-end repro output)**:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /root/main/openclaw/.worktrees/pr-85648
Test Files  7 passed (7)
     Tests  382 passed (382)
  Duration  86.07s

============ #85648 real-component repro ============
store entry.sessionStartedAt = 2026-05-23T04:02:30.699Z (post-/new cutoff)

--- BEFORE filter (raw read from real readRecentSessionMessagesAsync) ---
  role=user recordTs=2026-05-16T16:00:31.000Z text="[Inter-session message] sourceTool=subagent_announce isUser="
  role=assistant recordTs=2026-05-16T16:00:33.000Z text="Here's the fanfic lore-bible summary..."
  role=user recordTs=2026-05-23T04:03:10.000Z text="what's the agenda today?"

--- AFTER filter (real dropPreSessionStartAnnouncePairs) ---
  role=user recordTs=2026-05-23T04:03:10.000Z text="what's the agenda today?"

--- AFTER filter with sessionStartedAt=undefined (no-op path) ---
  count unchanged: true (same reference)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: the end-to-end harness proves the full data flow: (a) the **real** reader emits the new `__openclaw.recordTimestampMs` on every persisted message (`2026-05-16T16:00:31.000Z` / `2026-05-16T16:00:33.000Z` / `2026-05-23T04:03:10.000Z` exactly mirror the outer JSONL timestamps); (b) with the post-/new `sessionStartedAt` cutoff applied, the **real** filter removes the announce-user + adjacent assistant pair as a unit while the fresh-session turn passes through unchanged; (c) with `sessionStartedAt` undefined, the filter is a no-op and returns the same array reference. The unit test suite then pins each of the six branch axes (pre-cutoff drop, mid-session preserve, undefined cutoff no-op, trailing user-only drop, normal pre-cutoff preservation, missing record timestamp preservation) and the previously-failing `session-history-state.test.ts` "drops subagent announce inter-session user messages from projected history" case is restored to green (revert of the earlier projection-side attempt). No regression in the other `projectRecentChatDisplayMessages` / `sanitizeChatHistoryMessages` cases.
- **What was not tested**: a full live feishu/CLI session reset against a real lark account was not driven. The repro uses the production reader + production filter against on-disk JSONL with the production session-store entry shape (`sessionFile` + `sessionStartedAt`); the only un-exercised piece is the outer `chat.history` server-method wrapper (`loadSessionEntry` → schema validate → response shaping), which is independently covered by the existing `chat.history` server-method tests and is untouched by this PR.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: the new filter could over-strip a legitimate user/assistant pair, or under-strip and leave the bug.
- **Mitigation**: four gates protect against over-strip — (1) `entry.sessionStartedAt` must be set (otherwise the input is returned unchanged); (2) the user message must satisfy `provenance.kind === "inter_session" && provenance.sourceTool === "subagent_announce"` (a regular user message cannot match); (3) the message's `__openclaw.recordTimestampMs` must be finite and strictly less than the cutoff (missing or current-session timestamps are kept); (4) only the immediately-adjacent assistant — not later messages — is removed with the user announce. Under-strip is handled by the trailing-announce fall-through test (user-only announce is still hidden by the existing display projection).

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Sessions / storage

### Linked Issue/PR

Refs #85648 (announce sub-case only; see "What did NOT change" for the idle-reset supplementary sub-case left uncovered)

target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

### Control UI session evidence

The Control UI (`openclaw control`, default port 18789) shows the live
session backed by the bundled Copilot runtime. The `RUNTIME` column
reports `copilot` (the new SDK runtime) and the status reaches `Done`
after a full chat completion, confirming the end-to-end path through
the bridge:

![OpenClaw Control UI sessions view: agent:main:main session with kind=direct, status=Done, runtime=copilot, tokens 22340/128000](https://github.com/ramrajprabu/openclaw/releases/download/pr-86155-evidence/openclaw-sessions-evidence.png)

Session store (visible at the top of the panel):
`/root/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`
- the same on-disk store the existing PI path uses, so Control UI,
session resume, and usage accounting all work unchanged for hosts
that opt in.

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [x] Screenshot/recording: Control UI sessions view above shows the live `copilot` runtime session completing end-to-end (status `Done`, 22340/128000 tokens used).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left
untouched per `AGENTS.md` / ClawSweeper. Suggested changelog entry on
land:

> Agent runtimes: add the bundled `copilot` plugin as a peer agent
> runtime backed by `@github/copilot-sdk` and the GitHub Copilot CLI,
> selectable per-model or per-provider via
> `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"`; the canonical `github-copilot`
> provider is claimed by the runtime's doctor contract. PI remains the
> default and `auto` never selects `copilot`.

target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

### Control UI session evidence

The Control UI (`openclaw control`, default port 18789) shows the live
session backed by the bundled Copilot runtime. The `RUNTIME` column
reports `copilot` (the new SDK runtime) and the status reaches `Done`
after a full chat completion, confirming the end-to-end path through
the bridge:

![OpenClaw Control UI sessions view: agent:main:main session with kind=direct, status=Done, runtime=copilot, tokens 22340/128000](https://github.com/ramrajprabu/openclaw/releases/download/pr-86155-evidence/openclaw-sessions-evidence.png)

Session store (visible at the top of the panel):
`/root/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`
- the same on-disk store the existing PI path uses, so Control UI,
session resume, and usage accounting all work unchanged for hosts
that opt in.

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [x] Screenshot/recording: Control UI sessions view above shows the live `copilot` runtime session completing end-to-end (status `Done`, 22340/128000 tokens used).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left
untouched per `AGENTS.md` / ClawSweeper. Suggested changelog entry on
land:

> Agent runtimes: add the bundled `copilot` plugin as a peer agent
> runtime backed by `@github/copilot-sdk` and the GitHub Copilot CLI,
> selectable per-model or per-provider via
> `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"`; the canonical `github-copilot`
> provider is claimed by the runtime's doctor contract. PI remains the
> default and `auto` never selects `copilot`.

target.title: fix(ui): ignore stale running rows after terminal chat
target.body:
## Summary
- keep a terminal current chat session from being revived by stale `sessions.list` or `sessions.changed` rows with `status: "running"`
- preserve real newly started runs by allowing newer `startedAt` rows after the previous terminal `endedAt`
- add regressions for stale list rows, stale websocket rows, and late stale websocket rows after the run-status toast clears
- include a lint-only cleanup for redundant `image-tool.ts` provider-registry casts required by current `pnpm check:changed`

Fixes #86939.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Webchat can flip back from `Done` to permanent `In progress` when a stale current-session row with `status: "running"` arrives after the terminal chat event has already cleared the local `chatRunId`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `27ff166db9d6`, Linux, Node `v22.22.0`, real controller/runtime modules executed via `node --import tsx`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a current dashboard session row already terminal (`status: "done"`, `hasActiveRun: false`, `chatRunId: null`), applied a stale `sessions.changed` payload with `status: "running"` and `hasActiveRun: true`, then checked `hasAbortableSessionRun()`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the runtime command:

```text
environment=node v22.22.0 platform=linux
beforeAbortable=false
{
  "applied": true,
  "change": "updated"
}
{
  "key": "agent:main:dashboard",
  "kind": "direct",
  "updatedAt": 3,
  "hasActiveRun": false,
  "status": "done",
  "startedAt": 1,
  "endedAt": 2,
  "sessionId": "sess-main"
}
afterStaleEventAbortable=false
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The stale running update was applied for metadata freshness but normalized back to `hasActiveRun: false` and `status: "done"`, so abortable chat UI stays disabled after the terminal run-status toast clears.
- **What was not tested:** No live browser dashboard was driven manually; the proof uses the real session controller and chat abortability predicate, with UI/controller regression coverage below.

## Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

Note: running all three recommended UI test files in one `run-vitest` invocation timed out after 240s in this local environment with no failure output, but the three files passed when run individually.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim.
